core_mechanism: maximum-relative-entropy (minimum-KL-divergence) variational updating of a probability distribution subject to expectation/moment constraints, yielding an exponential-family 'canonical' posterior via Lagrange multipliers
brief_reason: The abstract explicitly uses the method of Maximum (relative) Entropy to fold observed data and moment constraints into a distribution, deriving the generic canonical form of the posterior, which is the constrained entropy-maximization inference principle.
